Question: What are the primary business challenges driving the Amatra agentic engine initiative?
Guidance: Describe the top pain points with the current manual EO Framework process — e.g. time per engagement, error rates, consultant bottlenecks
Notes: Core justification for the platform; aligns solution scope with measurable business value

Question: How many pre-sales engagements does Amatra currently run per month, and what is the target throughput after automation?
Guidance: Provide current monthly volume and desired steady-state (e.g. 200 solutions/month)
Notes: Drives quota design, concurrency limits, and infrastructure sizing

Question: What are the measurable success criteria for the proof-of-concept demonstration in Q2 2026?
Guidance: Examples: end-to-end generation under 60 minutes, 90% reduction in senior consultant hours, zero human-in-the-loop for standard artifacts
Notes: Defines done for Phase 3 and governs scope decisions

Question: What does a successful executive sponsor demonstration look like for Sarah Lin in April 2026?
Guidance: Identify the must-have capabilities vs. nice-to-have polish items for the demo
Notes: Prevents scope creep and focuses delivery on demo-critical path

Question: What is the current per-engagement senior consultant cost, and what is the target cost after automation?
Guidance: Current estimate is 6-10 hours at senior rates; quantify the dollar saving per engagement
Notes: Validates the business case and guides prioritisation of automation depth

Question: Please confirm the total budget envelope: ~$250k professional services and $100k-$200k first-year run cost. Are these figures approved?
Guidance: Confirm CapEx vs. OpEx split and any budget approval thresholds requiring CTO or Procurement sign-off
Notes: Scope and staffing decisions depend on confirmed budget; Procurement involvement if AWS spend exceeds existing envelope

Question: What is the per-solution model spend target, and how was the sub-$5 Bedrock token cost estimate derived?
Guidance: Confirm assumptions: artifact count, token counts per agent, Claude Sonnet 4.6 vs. Haiku 4.5 split
Notes: Cost model accuracy impacts pricing to end customers and platform viability

Question: Who are the decision-makers for CTO sign-off on the Cognito user pool and production deployment?
Guidance: Identify name, title, and approval timeline; confirm whether CTO is a blocker for Phase 1 or Phase 3
Notes: Stakeholder alignment critical for Cognito provisioning and production go-live gate

Question: Who from PREDICTif will be embedded in the delivery team, and what is their availability?
Guidance: Identify internal SMEs (pre-sales engineering, DevOps, security) and time commitment (full-time, part-time)
Notes: Resource planning for the 12-week engagement

Question: Are the Phase 1 (weeks 1-4), Phase 2 (weeks 5-9), and Phase 3 (weeks 10-12) milestone dates fixed or can they flex?
Guidance: Confirm hard vs. soft deadlines and any blackout periods (holidays, all-hands, existing commitments)
Notes: Milestone rigidity drives staffing plan and risk buffer

Question: Is the four-month PoC schedule tied to a contractual commitment with an end customer, or is it an internal delivery target?
Guidance: Clarify consequences of a delay — missed customer demo, contract penalty, or internal re-prioritisation
Notes: Determines contingency planning approach and scope-deferral authority

Question: Confirm the target AWS region is us-west-2. Are there any disaster-recovery or data-residency requirements that would require a secondary region?
Guidance: Note any cross-region replication needs for DynamoDB, S3, or Cognito
Notes: Region selection impacts latency, service availability, and compliance

Question: Describe the existing AWS account structure. Will the platform be deployed into a new standalone account or an existing AWS Organisation?
Guidance: Identify Organisation units, SCPs, and any guardrails that could restrict service usage (Bedrock, AgentCore, Lambda)
Notes: Account and OU design affects IAM, networking, and billing isolation

Question: Have AWS service quota increases been requested for Bedrock AgentCore Runtime, Lambda concurrency, and API Gateway?
Guidance: Check default concurrency limits; at 200 solutions/month with 5 agents each, burst capacity may exceed defaults
Notes: Quota increases can take days; must be requested early in Phase 1

Question: Confirm that Claude Sonnet 4.6 (generation) and Claude Haiku 4.5 (validation) are available in us-west-2 and that model access has been enabled in the AWS account.
Guidance: Bedrock model access requires explicit enablement per region; some models have waitlists
Notes: Model availability is a hard dependency for Phase 2 agent development

Question: What is the expected token volume per solution (input + output) across all five agents?
Guidance: Estimate tokens per artifact per agent; multiply by 12 artifacts and expected retry rate to size Bedrock quota
Notes: Token volume drives cost modelling and quota requests

Question: Confirm the five-agent topology: Input Validator, Pre-Sales Generator, Delivery Generator, Code Generator, EO Validator. Are these sequential or can any run in parallel?
Guidance: Identify agent dependency graph — which agents can be parallelised to meet the sub-60-minute SLA
Notes: Parallelisation design impacts AgentCore Runtime configuration and cost

Question: How are agent-to-agent messages passed? Via S3, DynamoDB, or an in-memory Strands context object?
Guidance: Clarify the state store design for intermediate artifacts and validation results
Notes: State management affects durability, retry logic, and debugging capability

Question: Provide the full list of 14 CLI subcommands and 11 Lambda routes. Which are in scope for the PoC vs. a future phase?
Guidance: Confirm the minimum viable API surface for the April demo
Notes: API surface scope directly drives Lambda development effort

Question: What is the expected API request volume at steady state and at peak (e.g. end-of-quarter sales pushes)?
Guidance: Estimate requests per day/hour and burst patterns
Notes: Sizing API Gateway throttle limits and Lambda reserved concurrency

Question: Confirm the Cognito User Pool configuration: 30-day refresh tokens, post-confirmation trigger Lambda writing to DynamoDB. Are social identity providers (Google, Microsoft SSO) required?
Guidance: Identify whether PREDICTif consultants will authenticate with corporate SSO (SAML/OIDC) or Cognito-native credentials
Notes: SSO integration adds scope; must be confirmed before Cognito design is finalised

Question: How many Cognito Monthly Active Users (MAUs) are anticipated at launch and at 12-month scale?
Guidance: Current team is ~120 consultants; will external partners or customers ever access the platform?
Notes: MAU count affects Cognito pricing tier and is a first-year run cost input

Question: What is the expected S3 storage volume for raw and converted artifacts at steady state (200 solutions/month)?
Guidance: Estimate: 12 artifacts × average artifact size × retention period
Notes: S3 cost and lifecycle policy design

Question: What is the DynamoDB access pattern for user profiles, quotas, and solution metadata? Confirm on-demand vs. provisioned capacity preference.
Guidance: At 200 solutions/month the write rate is low; confirm whether bursty sales activity could cause hot partitions
Notes: DynamoDB capacity mode selection and cost model

Question: Describe the eof-tools converter library: number of modules (~30), language versions, and any OS-level dependencies (e.g. LibreOffice, Pandoc).
Guidance: Confirm the base Docker image, image size, and ECR repository strategy (single image vs. per-agent images)
Notes: Container build pipeline design and ECR storage cost

Question: What is the CI/CD pipeline for building and pushing the agent container image to ECR?
Guidance: Identify tooling: GitHub Actions, AWS CodePipeline, or manual push; confirm image signing requirements
Notes: Automation of container builds is a Phase 2 deliverable

Question: Confirm the GitHub repository structure and the PAT (Personal Access Token) storage strategy. Will the PAT be stored in AWS Secrets Manager?
Guidance: Identify whether the target repo is org-level or user-level and the branch/PR strategy for committed artifacts
Notes: Security review required for PAT scope; Secrets Manager rotation policy needed

Question: What is the end-to-end generation SLA per solution (target: under 60 minutes)? Are there sub-SLAs per phase or per agent?
Guidance: Identify which agents are on the critical path and acceptable tail latency
Notes: Performance budgeting across agents; impacts retry timeout configuration

Question: Describe the per-user monthly quota (10 solutions) and global pool (1,000 solutions/month) enforcement mechanism. How are atomic DynamoDB increments implemented?
Guidance: Confirm whether optimistic locking or conditional writes are used; identify quota reset cadence (calendar month vs. rolling 30 days)
Notes: Quota logic is a core platform feature; must be bulletproof before production

Question: What Terraform version and provider versions are required? Is Terraform Cloud or AWS CodeBuild used for plan/apply?
Guidance: Confirm `terraform validate` as the PoC gate; identify whether `terraform plan` and `apply` are in scope for Phase 3
Notes: IaC scope and pipeline design for the automation bundle artifact

Question: Are there any integrations beyond GitHub and AWS services (e.g. Jira for ticket creation, Slack for notifications, CRM for opportunity tracking)?
Guidance: Identify any webhooks or event-driven integrations required for Phase 1 or Phase 3
Notes: Additional integrations add scope; must be scoped explicitly

Question: What compliance frameworks apply to the Amatra platform? (e.g. SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, CMMC, FedRAMP)
Guidance: PREDICTif operates as an AWS partner serving enterprise customers; identify any inherited compliance obligations
Notes: Compliance posture drives security control selection and audit logging requirements

Question: What is the highest data classification of content processed by the platform?
Guidance: Consider client briefings, solution artifacts, and any PII in user profiles; classify as Public / Internal / Confidential / Restricted
Notes: Data classification determines encryption, access control, and retention requirements

Question: What IAM permission boundaries and Service Control Policies (SCPs) apply in the target AWS account?
Guidance: Identify any deny-list SCPs that could block Bedrock, AgentCore, Cognito, or Lambda cross-account access
Notes: SCP conflicts can block deployment; must be reviewed in Phase 1

Question: What is the IAM role strategy for agents? Will each agent (Input Validator, Pre-Sales Generator, etc.) have a dedicated least-privilege role?
Guidance: Identify whether roles are per-agent or shared; confirm cross-service permissions (Bedrock, S3, DynamoDB, Secrets Manager)
Notes: Least-privilege IAM design is a security best practice and audit requirement

Question: What encryption requirements apply to data at rest and in transit?
Guidance: Confirm: S3 SSE-KMS vs. SSE-S3, DynamoDB encryption, Secrets Manager KMS key, TLS 1.2+ for API Gateway
Notes: Encryption key management strategy affects cost and operational complexity

Question: Will a customer-managed KMS key (CMK) be required, or is AWS-managed encryption sufficient?
Guidance: CMK provides audit trail and key rotation control but adds operational overhead
Notes: CMK requirement driven by compliance framework and data sensitivity

Question: How will the GitHub PAT, Bedrock API keys, and any third-party credentials be managed?
Guidance: Confirm AWS Secrets Manager with automatic rotation where supported; identify rotation frequency
Notes: Hardcoded credentials are a critical risk; Secrets Manager is mandatory

Question: What VPC and network segmentation requirements apply?
Guidance: Confirm whether Lambda functions and AgentCore Runtime must run inside a VPC; identify VPC endpoint requirements for S3, DynamoDB, Bedrock
Notes: VPC configuration affects latency and egress costs; some Bedrock features require VPC endpoints

Question: What audit logging and trail requirements exist?
Guidance: Confirm: CloudTrail for API calls, CloudWatch Logs for Lambda/agent execution, S3 access logging, Cognito sign-in events
Notes: Audit trail is required for SOC 2 and incident response; retention period must be specified

Question: What is the process for patching the agent container image (base OS, Python packages, eof-tools)?
Guidance: Identify ECR image scanning (Basic vs. Inspector), patch cadence, and responsible team
Notes: Container vulnerabilities are the primary attack surface; scanning policy needed

Question: Must all data (S3 artifacts, DynamoDB records, CloudWatch logs) remain within us-west-2?
Guidance: Confirm no cross-region replication unless explicitly approved; check CloudWatch Logs Insights cross-region queries
Notes: Data residency confirmation needed before architecture is finalised

Question: What are the Recovery Time Objective (RTO) and Recovery Point Objective (RPO) requirements for the Amatra platform?
Guidance: Example: RTO < 4 hours, RPO < 1 hour. Consider DynamoDB point-in-time recovery and S3 versioning
Notes: DR architecture depends on RTO/RPO targets; influences multi-AZ vs. multi-region design

Question: What monitoring and alerting capabilities are required for the platform?
Guidance: Identify key metrics: agent execution duration, retry rates, quota utilisation, API error rates, Bedrock throttling
Notes: CloudWatch dashboard and alarm configuration scope

Question: Who receives operational alerts (PagerDuty, SNS email, Slack) and what are the escalation paths?
Guidance: Identify on-call ownership — Amatra internal team or PREDICTif managed services
Notes: Alert routing design and on-call runbook scope

Question: What support coverage is required for the platform: business hours (8x5) or 24x7?
Guidance: Consider the sales cycle — do engagements run outside business hours, e.g. overnight batch generation?
Notes: Support model drives staffing and AWS Support plan tier selection

Question: What backup strategy is required for DynamoDB (user profiles, quotas) and S3 (solution artifacts)?
Guidance: Confirm DynamoDB PITR (35-day retention), S3 versioning, and any cross-region backup requirements
Notes: Backup scope and retention period drive storage costs

Question: How will the platform scale beyond 200 solutions/month if demand grows?
Guidance: Identify scaling triggers: Lambda auto-scaling, DynamoDB auto-scaling, Bedrock quota increase SLA
Notes: Growth planning ensures the architecture is not a ceiling; important for investor/customer demos

Question: How will per-solution cost be tracked and reported (Bedrock tokens, Lambda invocations, S3 operations)?
Guidance: Identify whether AWS Cost Explorer tags per solution ID, or whether a custom cost-attribution mechanism is needed
Notes: Per-solution cost tracking validates the sub-$5 model spend target and enables customer billing

Question: What maintenance windows are available for patching, schema migrations, and container image updates?
Guidance: Identify blackout periods (e.g. end-of-quarter sales pushes) and acceptable downtime
Notes: Maintenance window planning affects deployment pipeline design

Question: What operational training is required for the Amatra team to manage the platform post-delivery?
Guidance: Identify runbook requirements, CloudWatch dashboard training, and incident response procedures
Notes: Knowledge transfer scope and timeline for Phase 3

Question: What is the cutover strategy from the current manual Claude Code CLI process to the automated platform?
Guidance: Options: hard cutover on go-live date, parallel run (both old and new), or phased by artifact type
Notes: Migration approach affects timeline and consultant re-training scope

Question: Will existing manually-generated solution artifacts be migrated into the new S3/DynamoDB store, or will the platform start fresh?
Guidance: Estimate volume of historical artifacts and metadata to migrate
Notes: Data migration scope impacts Phase 3 effort

Question: What is the maximum acceptable downtime for the platform during go-live cutover?
Guidance: Given no current SLA, is any downtime acceptable, or does the sales team require zero-downtime deployment?
Notes: Determines blue/green vs. rolling deployment strategy

Question: Is a parallel run required where both the manual process and the automated platform run simultaneously?
Guidance: Specify duration (e.g. 2 weeks) and acceptance criteria for cutting off the manual process
Notes: Parallel run adds effort but reduces risk; drives Phase 3 timeline

Question: Which PREDICTif team members will be available for the 12-week engagement, and what are their roles and weekly time commitments?
Guidance: Identify: AWS architect, DevOps engineer, pre-sales SME, QA/testing resource
Notes: Resource gaps drive subcontracting decisions and timeline risk

Question: How many environments are required: development, staging, production? Will each have its own AWS account?
Guidance: Confirm environment isolation strategy and whether staging mirrors production (same Bedrock models, same Cognito pool)
Notes: Environment design affects IAM, cost, and CI/CD pipeline complexity

Question: What user acceptance testing (UAT) approach is planned? Who signs off on each phase milestone?
Guidance: Identify UAT participants (Marcus Patel, Daniel Park), test scenarios, and acceptance criteria per artifact type
Notes: UAT gate is required before Phase 3 sign-off and executive demo

Question: What go-live readiness checklist items must be completed before the April 2026 executive demo?
Guidance: Examples: all 5 presales artifacts passing validation, CLI auth working, quota enforcement tested, CloudWatch baseline green
Notes: Go-live criteria define the finish line and prevent scope creep

Question: How will the 120 Amatra consultants be onboarded to the new CLI and API?
Guidance: Identify training format (self-service docs, live session, video walkthrough) and rollout plan
Notes: Change management is a delivery risk; early planning reduces adoption friction

Question: Are there any AWS program credits (MAP, APN, EDP) or Bedrock committed-use agreements available to offset first-year run costs?
Guidance: Confirm existing AWS EDP agreement status and eligibility for MAP credits for migration workloads
Notes: Credits can materially reduce the $100k-$200k first-year run cost estimate

Question: What is the risk if AWS Bedrock AgentCore Runtime is not generally available or has breaking changes before the April 2026 deadline?
Guidance: Identify fallback options: self-hosted Strands on ECS, Step Functions orchestration, or Lambda-native orchestration
Notes: AgentCore Runtime is a new service; GA timeline and API stability must be validated

Question: What is the contingency if Claude Sonnet 4.6 or Haiku 4.5 are not available in us-west-2 by the PoC deadline?
Guidance: Confirm model availability schedule with AWS account team; identify acceptable substitute models
Notes: Model availability is a hard dependency; early confirmation reduces go/no-go risk

Question: What external dependencies could delay Phase 1 (weeks 1-4)? Examples: AWS account provisioning, CTO approval for Cognito, Procurement for spend envelope.
Guidance: Identify owner and expected lead time for each dependency
Notes: Phase 1 dependencies on the critical path must be tracked from day one

Question: Are there any organisational or policy constraints on storing client briefing data (containing engagement IP) in S3?
Guidance: Confirm data classification of input briefs and whether any customers have contractual restrictions on cloud storage
Notes: Data handling constraints may require additional encryption or access controls

Question: Confirm the assumption that the eof-tools converter library (~30 modules) is stable and will not require significant re-engineering during the engagement.
Guidance: Identify known technical debt, unsupported artifact types, or planned refactoring
Notes: Unstable eof-tools would increase Phase 2 effort significantly
